steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content: The first part is an independent clause: “The sand at Talofofo Beach in Guam is an exception.” After the blank comes another clause starting with “sand gets its shade from deposits...” that needs to be properly connected.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule for dependent clauses
steps.content:
A comma must precede a subordinating conjunction when introducing a dependent clause after an independent clause. Choice A (“exception, though most”) correctly places the comma before “though,” while the other choices either use wrong punctuation marks (B's colon, D's period) or create ungrammatical structure (C's parenthetical commas).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
